title: France, Norway and more teams advance to the World Cup round of 16
summary: The knockout phase of the World Cup continues with victories by France, Norway, Morocco and Paraguay.

title: Reference land prices in Japan record biggest increase in five years
summary: Land valuations across the country rise 2.9% for the fifth consecutive year, driven by tourism and housing demand.

title: Nikkei index surges more than 1,500 points on tech stock rally
summary: The Tokyo stock market gets a strong boost after the rebound on Wall Street, surpassing 71,500 yen.

title: Dow Jones hits new all-time high driven by AI
summary: The New York index rises for the second consecutive day, closing above 52,000 points.

title: Yen falls to its lowest level in 40 years against the dollar
summary: The Japanese currency surpasses 162 units per dollar, pressured by structural factors and monetary policy.

title: Business confidence among large Japanese manufacturers improves for fifth quarter
summary: The Bank of Japan's diffusion index shows significant improvement in the manufacturing and services sectors.

title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship
summary: The high court rejects Trump's executive order that sought to restrict automatic citizenship for children of undocumented immigrants.

title: Japanese government presents reform of the Imperial House Law
summary: The proposal maintains exclusive male succession, sparking debate on gender equality in the monarchy.

title: US lifts export restrictions on Anthropic AI
summary: The US administration allows the resumption of supply of 'Mythos' artificial intelligence models after reviewing control regulations.

title: Japan's ruling party uses majority to pass key laws in Parliament
summary: The governing coalition forces a vote on seat reduction and the second capital bill amid strong criticism from the opposition.

title: Japan, UK and Italy agree on budget for next-generation fighter jet development
summary: The three countries allocate 1.85 trillion yen for the joint fighter jet program, aiming to strengthen their defense capabilities.

title: Japan and Mercosur begin negotiations for a free trade agreement
summary: The South American bloc and Japan agree to start formal talks to deepen economic cooperation.

title: Keiko Fujimori wins presidential election in Peru
summary: The Popular Force candidate wins the elections and receives congratulations from US Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

title: Chubu Electric manipulated safety data at Hamaoka nuclear power plant
summary: The electric power company falsified information on safety inspections even after the nuclear regulator's investigation began.

title: Japan and India strengthen economic and security cooperation
summary: The Japanese Prime Minister agrees with India on the construction of 1,000 biogas plants and greater collaboration in trade and defense.

title: Footballer Tanaka Midori expresses regret after Japan's defeat to Brazil
summary: The Japanese midfielder cries in the post-match interview and says 'only the World Cup can heal the pain of the World Cup'.

title: Oil falls in markets and Japan stresses the need to secure energy supply
summary: Crude oil prices retreat on expectations of increased supply, while the Japanese government highlights the urgency of building a solid energy economy.

title: Japan promotes use of artificial intelligence in legal and business sectors
summary: The Ministry of Justice considers easing regulations for AI legal consulting, while the government supports the development of domestic AI with an investment of 1 trillion yen.

title: Honinbo and Ryūō: key events in the world of go and shogi
summary: Important matches are held in go and shogi tournaments, with the best players competing for the titles.

title: Trump announces Republican convention will be held in September
summary: The former US president confirms the date of the party event, which will be held before the midterm elections for the first time in history.
